tests.reason: A 5.509 Msun MS star would have L ~ 700 Lsun and Teff ~ 16000 K (mid-B type).  Against the K-giant primary (L ~ 600 Lsun), it would contribute ~45% of the G-band flux and dominate in the BP band.  The observed single-star SED with BP-RP = 1.77 (pure cool star) excludes a luminous hot companion.
M2 = 5.509 Msun exceeds the Chandrasekhar limit (1.44 Msun) by a factor of 3.8.  No known single WD can reach this mass.

M2 = 5.509 Msun exceeds the TOV limit (2.3 Msun) by a factor of 2.4.  Even the most generous NS EOS (M_max ~ 2.5 Msun) cannot accommodate this mass. Note: the posterior has a tail into the mass gap (3-5 Msun) due to M1 uncertainty, but the median remains above the NS ceiling.

Stability requires P_inner < 19.6 d.  Two ~ 2.8 Msun MS stars would have combined L ~ 130 Lsun (early A-type) — detectable in the SED.  If both are compact objects (WDs/NSs), each at ~ 2.8 Msun exceeds the Chandrasekhar limit.  A compact triple with two BHs is astrophysically contrived.

A 5.509 Msun stripped He star at Teff > 40000 K would produce strong UV emission.  No GALEX detection is confirmed at this position.  The absence of X-ray emission in ROSAT is consistent with a quiescent BH but does not definitively exclude a hot subdwarf.  UV spectroscopy would close this channel.

The Orbital solution has significance 28.0 (5.6x the threshold of 5).  RUWE = 5.4 is moderately elevated, consistent with a binary.  EN_sig = 692 indicates coherent excess astrometric noise.  GoF = 8.17 flags imperfect model fit, which is common for bright sources with complex scan angle coverage.  El-Badry+2024 notes that ~20% of Orbital solutions fail RV checks, so independent RV confirmation is essential.

The orbital solution represents a coherent Keplerian signal at 28 sigma over 444 d.  At G = 6.47 (very bright), blending with a background source producing periodic astrometric perturbation is extremely unlikely.  The high eccentricity (e = 0.583) is consistent with a genuine binary.

conclusion: BH CANDIDATE: All standard non-BH scenarios are excluded or disfavoured.  The borderline mass (M2 = 5.509 Msun) places this object at the lower edge of the stellar-mass BH range.  RV confirmation is critical to validate the Orbital solution.
